purpose.summary: The DBUNLDGS program extracts authorization summary and detail data from an IMS database and inserts it into a GSAM dataset. It reads authorization summary records and then reads associated detail records, writing each to the GSAM file.
purpose.business_context: This program likely supports reporting or auditing of authorization data by extracting it from the operational IMS database into a format suitable for analysis.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: The IMS database PCB used to access authorization summary and detail segments.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: The IMS segment containing authorization summary data.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: The IMS segment containing authorization detail data.

inputs.name: PASFLPCB
inputs.description: The GSAM PCB used to write the extracted data.

outputs.name: GSAM Output
outputs.description: A GSAM dataset containing extracted authorization summary and detail records.

called_programs.purpose: Abends the program when an error occurs.
Abends the program when an error occurs.
Abends the program when an error occurs.

business_rules.description: The program only inserts the parent segment into the GSAM file if the PA-ACCT-ID is numeric.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks if PA-ACCT-ID is numeric before calling 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph defines the program-id. It does not contain any executable code.

This is the main control paragraph of the DBUNLDGS program. It is the entry point ('DLITCBL') and receives three PCBs (PAUTBPCB, PASFLPCB, PADFLPCB) via the USING clause. It first calls 1000-INITIALIZE to perform initialization tasks. Then, it enters a loop that continues as long as WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG is not equal to 'Y'. Inside the loop, it calls 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY to retrieve the next authorization summary record. After processing all summary records, it calls 4000-FILE-CLOSE to close the GSAM output file. Finally, it executes a GOBACK statement to return control to the calling program.

This paragraph initializes the program. It accepts the current date and day from the system. It then displays a series of messages to the console, including the program name, a separator line, and the current date. The purpose is to provide basic program start-up information and a timestamp for debugging and monitoring purposes. It doesn't read any input files or produce any output files. It does not call any other paragraphs.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph (MAIN-PARA). It does not perform any data manipulation or error handling.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization summary record from the IMS database. It initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS and then calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GN to retrieve the next root segment (authorization summary). If the call is successful (PAUT-PCB-STATUS = SPACES), it increments counters WS-NO-SUMRY-READ and WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT, moves the retrieved data to OPFIL1-REC, initializes ROOT-SEG-KEY and CHILD-SEG-REC, moves PA-ACCT-ID to ROOT-SEG-KEY, and then, if PA-ACCT-ID is numeric, calls 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM to insert the parent segment into the GSAM file and then calls 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL to retrieve the child segments. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is 'GB', it sets END-OF-AUTHDB to TRUE and moves 'Y' to WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG to terminate the main processing loop. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is neither spaces nor 'GB', it displays error messages and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph (MAIN-PARA). It does not perform any data manipulation or error handling.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization detail record (child segment) from the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GNP to retrieve the next child segment. If the call is successful (PAUT-PCB-STATUS = SPACES), it sets MORE-AUTHS to TRUE, increments counters WS-NO-SUMRY-READ and WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT, moves the retrieved data to CHILD-SEG-REC, and calls 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM to insert the child segment into the GSAM file. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is 'GE', it sets NO-MORE-AUTHS to TRUE and moves 'Y' to WS-END-OF-CHILD-SEG to signal the end of child segments for the current parent. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is neither spaces nor 'GE', it displays error messages and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. Finally, it initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph (2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY). It does not perform any data manipulation or error handling.

This paragraph inserts the parent segment (authorization summary) into the GSAM dataset. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-ISRT to perform the insertion. If the GSAM call is not successful (PASFL-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES), it displays error messages and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph relies on the PASFLPCB, which represents the GSAM PCB, to manage the output file. The data to be written is assumed to be already present in PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph (2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY). It does not perform any data manipulation or error handling.

error_handling.condition: PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GB'
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GE'
